creator: gwright@antiope.com
text:
I have poked around a bit to try to understand the error.  I checked what I
believed to be the offending table and sequence and they appear to have the same
owner ('git').  I notice that most tables in the gitlabhq_production are owned
by 'git' but there are some that are owned by 'postgres'.

The sequence and table information:


gitlabhq_production=# select * from pg_sequences where sequencename='security_findings_id_seq';
 schemaname |       sequencename       | sequenceowner | data_type | start_value | min_value |      max_value      | increment_by | cycle | cache_size | last_value
------------+--------------------------+---------------+-----------+-------------+-----------+---------------------+--------------+-------+------------+------------
 public     | security_findings_id_seq | git           | bigint    |           1 |         1 | 9223372036854775807 |            1 | f     |          1 |
(1 row)

gitlabhq_production=# select * from pg_tables where tablename='security_findings';
 schemaname |     tablename     | tableowner | tablespace | hasindexes | hasrules | hastriggers | rowsecurity
------------+-------------------+------------+------------+------------+----------+-------------+-------------
 public     | security_findings | git        |            | t          | f        | t           | f
(1 row)
